game_name: Ayrton Senna's Super Monaco GP II

danger_signals.meaning: collision hazard; original game reduced speed when hitting stripes, removed in sequel per Senna's input

danger_signals.visual_form: rear-view display at screen top showing trailing vehicles
danger_signals.meaning: collision risk from vehicles behind; spatial awareness cue

danger_signals.visual_form: tachometer on left side, speedometer readout
danger_signals.meaning: engine strain and loss-of-control risk; communicates mechanical danger

safety_signals.visual_form: map display on right side of screen
safety_signals.meaning: navigation aid; shows safe racing line and upcoming turns
game communicates control vs. speed tradeoff; automatic = safer, manual = faster but riskier
mechanical readiness check; failure to qualify results in 12th place penalty, creating safe baseline

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: 15 computer-controlled opponents in World Championship mode
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: player-named car visible from driver's seat perspective; driver seat camera is player's faction POV

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: generic F1 cars without individual visual differentiation documented
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: rear-view display shows trailing opponent vehicles; 16-race championship structure implies persistent rivalry

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 19 recreated race tracks from 1991 Formula One World Championship calendar
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: 16-race season structure with qualifying, points competition, and championship progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: game title features Ayrton Senna name prominently; Senna recorded voice segments for each track
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Senna GP mode offers special tracks and 'Ayrton Senna Super License' achievement; endorsement reflects world champion credibility

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Circuit de Catalunya excluded initially (under construction during 1991); added post-opening via voice segment recording
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: world state authenticity maintained through delayed content; reflects real-world F1 calendar changes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Beginner vs. Master difficulty modes with visual/mechanical differences
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Beginner: 3 laps, easier competition; Master: 6 laps, harder AI; world feels more dangerous/complex at higher difficulty

environmental_storytelling.example: 19 tracks based on 1991 F1 season venues; each track's unique layout tells story of real racing locations
driver's-seat view with rear-view mirror and dashboard displays communicates lived experience of F1 driver
tachometer and speedometer indicate engine state; transmission choice (auto/semi/manual) shows mechanical complexity of real F1
Ayrton Senna recorded personal commentary on each track, adding narrative authority and championship-era context
16-race World Championship mode mirrors real F1 championship format; cumulative scoring implies political/competitive stakes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: easier controls but reduced top speed; visually manifests as slower acceleration indicators on speedometer
greater speed potential but requires precise gear management; tachometer becomes critical feedback tool
forced to start 12th place; visual penalty communicated via race grid position before race begins
difficulty scaling across 16-race season; opponents shown in rear-view indicate competitive adaptation
original game reduced speed on stripes; sequel removes this penalty per Senna's direction, changing danger signal

notes: This is a racing paradigm game with minimal faction/political complexity. World-state communication centers on F1 championship authenticity, mechanical readiness, and track navigation. Danger signals are primarily mechanical (speed, collisions) rather than narrative. Safety signals communicate control/difficulty tradeoffs. The game's primary manifestation method is dashboard-driven HUD communication (tachometer, speedometer, map) plus authentic track recreations. Senna's personal involvement adds narrative authority to championship legitimacy. No visual faction markers or conflict systems documented; competition is purely mechanical/positional.
